The bioactive constituents of Timut include compounds such as timutine, zanthoxylum, and limonoids, which have been shown to have various health benefits. In traditional Chinese medicine, Timut is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions.
